Input buffer
HP, -3db @40Hz
Clipping around 6.2V vs 0.5v, gain of 12.4 (21.8db)
TODO adjust for symmetric clipping


Muff double clipping stage
Input HP: -3db@20Hz
Tune feedback caps for LP cutoff
Smaller makes sharper at low freqs
At 10n it's basically a triangle wave
Tune emitter resistor for gain (1-1ktrim?)
Swap transistors and diodes
Switch bypass second stage
TODO adjust for symmetric clipping


1st order low pass, 20-200Hz
Avoids rumble

Bandpass 200-800Hz boost or buck
Adds fatness and power. Vintage=boost, modern scoop

Boost 1.5kHz-2.5kHz (flexible Q and gain)
Punch and clarity

Notch 4-6KHz
Removes whistle

Hi Q low pass with a peak around 5-7 kHz
Boost for presence but avoid harshness


1. Start with EQ pedal
2. Test mixing in an EQ of clean tone
3. Recreate with circuits


Idea: Bode magnitude plot but with N higher harmonics plotted also. Could analyze this plot across common tones, draw in software and simulate



----


sample_rate = 44100


20-20k => 20*pow(10, 1..3)
1000 samples linear spaced 1 to 3 inclusive


value of first 5 odd-order harmonics

n, 3n, 5n, 7n, 9n, 11n

bins: [
	n/2 : 2n,
	2n : 4n,
	4n : 6n,
	6n : 8n,
	8n : 10n,
	10n : 12n
]


realFFT( samples size N )
normalized = abs(s) / N

db = 10*log10(v)
